page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 360.0 GB of dedicated system memory, translating to 3.75 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core capabilities are highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU extended instructions testing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with cached read-only bandwidth reaching 3918.64 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth at 92.34 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Redis SET operations show strong performance at 62.03 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its weak single-threaded execution and lower-tier text generation speeds for large language models. However, its massive core count and strong memory bandwidth make it highly effective for parallelized workloads. It is well-suited for multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. The qualitative cost efficiency of the n1-standard-96 lies in its high resource density, allowing users to run highly parallelized legacy x86_64 workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 96 vCPUs and 360.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation and hyper-threading. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a significant divergence: single-core execution and text generation speeds are weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are exceptionally strong, placing in the top 25%. Specifically, cached read-only memory bandwidth reaches 3918.64 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations perform at 62.03 million operations per second. This hardware profile makes the instance highly effective for parallelized workloads such as multi-threaded database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, offering high resource density for non-GPU compute tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, making it suitable for highly parallelized workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-96 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 360 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 360 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth in the top 25%
Weak single-core execution and slow text generation for large language models
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for parallelized workloads, database operations, and software compilation
